preamble: The shift from the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j] in similar phonetic environments) to the voiceless post-alveolar affricate [tʃ] (represented here as 'ch') is a form of yod-coalescence and palatalization, typically occurring in connected speech or through historical mergers.

examples.note: Common coalescence in fluent speech where the terminal alveolar stop and initial palatal segment merge into an affricate.
Historical palatalization of dental stops triggered by the following palatal glide/vowel reflex.
